title: Koeleria koidzumiana
intro: Koeleria koidzumiana, also known as Japanese hair grass, is a perennial grass species native to East Asia. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of various conditions such as arthritis and respiratory diseases. The bioactive constituents of Koeleria koidzumiana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. Koeleria koidzumiana is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into traditional Chinese medicine formulas to support overall well-being and immune function.
taxonomy: Koeleria koidzumiana is classified as a species of grass within the family Poaceae. It is a member of the tribe Poeae and the subfamily Pooideae. The genus Koeleria is a group of grasses commonly known as hair grasses. Koeleria koidzumiana is native to East Asia, where it typically grows in mountainous regions. The exact classification hierarchy may vary depending on the source and the most recent taxonomic research.
